title: Per-record try/except inside batch DB loop breaks transaction atomicity
description: LLMs generate batch DB write loops with per-record try/except that swallow errors and continue, leaving the database in a partially-written state. The model optimizes for 'resilient' code at the expense of data consistency. The correct pattern is a single transaction wrapping the entire batch — all-or-nothing.
hallucination_signal: LLMs associate 'robustness' with per-item error handling and 'logging + continue' patterns. They don't model the invariant that partial writes corrupt downstream aggregations, and they rarely consider the transaction boundary as the unit of correctness.

detection_rule.context: loop body contains both .commit() and except: continue
detection_rule.message: Per-record commit inside loop breaks atomicity — wrap entire batch in a single transaction
detection_rule.fix: Use 'with session.begin():' wrapping the loop body; commit once after all inserts

real_world_instance.source: deep_think_mcp worker.py batch write pattern (store.py lines 378-392)
